title: Integrative extraction of primary metabolites and proteins from Deinococcus radiodurans
author: Emanuel Ott
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.j3bcqin
materials: Homogenize in bead beater (e.g. Roche MagNA Lyser 4x 7000 rpm, 30 sec; chill between cycles)
Homogenize one more time in the bead beater to dissolve pellet completely (Roche MagNA Lyser 1x 7000 rpm, 30 sec)
Last aspiration to total dryness, ~1 min.

steps.instruction: 1. Grow Deinococcus radiodurans in suitable medium (like TGB or TGY) in sufficient amount for the experiment - TGB recipe: 1 % tryptone, 0.6 % beef extract, 0.2 % glucose

steps.title: Centrifuge at 3000 g / 4...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 3000 g / 4 °C / 3 min (in Falcon tubes)

steps.title: Discard supernatant and dissolve pellet in 1
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant and dissolve pellet in 1.8 mL cold PBS (transfer to homogenization tube)

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1500 g / 4 °C / 3 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500 g / 4 °C / 3 min

steps.title: Repeat step 2 and 3 two times
steps.instruction: Repeat step 2 and 3 two times

steps.title: Discard washing solution and resuspend pellet...
steps.instruction: Discard washing solution and resuspend pellet in 1 mL cold, freshly prepared MCW (methanol 2.5 : chloroform 1 : water 0.

steps.title: Agitate for 10 sec, incubate 15...
steps.instruction: Agitate for 10 sec, incubate 15 min on ice (shake once a while)

steps.title: Centrifuge 21000 g / 4 °C / 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 21000 g / 4 °C / 5 min

steps.title: Separate supernatant (contains metabolites) from pellet...
steps.instruction: Separate supernatant (contains metabolites) from pellet (contains proteins and nucleic acids)

steps.title: Add 400 µL water to the supernatant (phase separation)
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL water to the supernatant (phase separation)

steps.title: Agitate shortly and centrifuge at 14000 g / 2 min
steps.instruction: Agitate shortly and centrifuge at 14000 g / 2 min

steps.title: Transfer upper (polar) phase into a...
steps.instruction: Transfer upper (polar) phase into a new Eppendorf tube (lower, apolar phase can be stored if desired)

steps.title: Dry samples in speed-vac by using...
steps.instruction: Dry samples in speed-vac by using the following gradient %s %s 200 mbar 30 min 120 mbar 30 min 80 mbar 30 min 50 mbar 30 min 20 mbar 30 min 0.001 mbar 3-4 h

steps.title: Store overnight at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Store overnight at -20 °C

steps.title: Resuspend Pellet in Trizol
steps.instruction: Resuspend Pellet in Trizol

steps.title: Incubate on a turning wheel at RT / 5 min
steps.instruction: Incubate on a turning wheel at RT / 5 min

steps.title: Add 200 µL Chloroform and shake vigorously for 15 sec
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL Chloroform and shake vigorously for 15 sec

steps.title: Incubate on a turning wheel at RT / 2-3 min
steps.instruction: Incubate on a turning wheel at RT / 2-3 min

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm / 4 °C / 15 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm / 4 °C / 15 min

steps.title: Remove aquaous phase (contains nucleic acids)
steps.instruction: Remove aquaous phase (contains nucleic acids)

steps.title: Add 400 µL water and incubate...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL water and incubate on turning wheel at RT / 2-3 min

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm / 4...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm / 4 °C / 15 min and remove aquaous phase

steps.title: Precipitate the proteins by adding 5x...
steps.instruction: Precipitate the proteins by adding 5x 100 mM ammonium acetate in MeOH

steps.title: Incubate overnight at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight at -20 °C

steps.title: Acclimate sample at room temperature when...
steps.instruction: Acclimate sample at room temperature when using frozen samples to avoid condensation

steps.title: Redissolve sample in methoximation reagent (40mg...
steps.instruction: Redissolve sample in methoximation reagent (40mg Methoxyaminhydrochlorid (CH 3 ONH 2 *HCL) in 1ml Pyridine) for methoximation

steps.title: Incubate 90 min
steps.instruction: Incubate 90 min. at 30°C using a thermo shaker

steps.title: Add 80 µL MSTFA (N-Methyl-N-(trimethylsilyl) trifluoroacetamide) for silylation
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL MSTFA (N-Methyl-N-(trimethylsilyl) trifluoroacetamide) for silylation

steps.title: Incubate for 30 min at 37 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 min at 37 °C

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14000 g / 2 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14000 g / 2 min

steps.title: Transfer supernatant in GC- microvials and...
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant in GC- microvials and close them with crimp caps Metabolites ready for GC-MS measurement

steps.title: Centrifuge 5000 g / 15 min / 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5000 g / 15 min / 4 °C. Carefully discard the supernatant by pipetting it out. Wash the pellet with 1 mL of acetone. Pipette up and down or use a soft ultrasound bath to disaggregate the pellet. Centrifuge 5000 g / 5 min / 4 °C. Discard the supernatant. Repeat step 2 two times. Air-dry the pellets until the acetone is completely eliminated. Store at -20 °C overnight

steps.title: Resuspend protein pellet in 8 M Urea, 4% (w/v) SDS
steps.instruction: Resuspend protein pellet in 8 M Urea, 4% (w/v) SDS. ( Recommend starting with the addition of a low volume of buffer, 20-30μL. If the buffer become very viscous (you can invert the tube and nothing is coming down), or there is some undissolved proteins more buffer can be added. A complete pellet solubilisation may take up to 3 h in a thermal shaker (35 °C and 750 rpm), being also some occasional pipetting needed.) C entrifuge at 21000 g / 5 min to remove insoluble particles. Perform a BCA with a standard curve  BCA is compatible with SDS, Bradford not. But no reducing substances like DTT or β-mercaptoethanol should be in the sample! Prepare Standard Working Reagent (SWR): Mix 100 vol. of reagent A with 2 vol. of reagent B. The solution is stable at room temperature for 1 week. BCA Reagent A: [sodium bicinchoninate (0.1 g), Na 2 CO 3 · H 2 O (2.0 g), sodium tartrate (dihydrate) (0.16 g), NaOH (0.4 g), NaHCO 3 (0.95 g), made up to 100 mL. If necessary, adjust the pH to 11.25 with NaHCO 3 or NaOH] BCA Reagent B: [CuSO 4 5·H 2 O (0.4 g) in 10 mL of water] Reagents A and B are stable indefinitely at RT. Add BSA standards to an ELISA plate. 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 10, 15 and 20 μg of BSA (in the same buffer as sample is dissolved). Three technical triplicates are advised. Add 2 μL of sample to each well. Add 200 μL of SWR to each well. Shake the plate gently. Close the plate with a cap or aluminium foil to minimize evaporation. Incubate the plate 20 min at 60 °C (in an oven) or 40 min at 45 °C in the plate reader. Read absorbance at 562 nm.

steps.title: Prepare SDS-PAGE Gels 10x Running Buffer (TGS)
steps.instruction: Prepare SDS-PAGE Gels 10x Running Buffer (TGS): [10 g of SDS, 30.2 g Tris-Base, 144 g of glycine, add mQH 2 O up to 1 L] Resolving gel: [12.5 % acrylamide:bisacrylamide, 0.1 % SDS, 0.375 M Tris-HCl pH 8.8, 0.2 % TEMED, 0.073 % APS.] Avoid bubbles during casting and quickly cover the acrylamide with 2-propanol. Let the gels polymerize for 1 hour. Stacking gel: [5 % acrylamide:bisacrylamide, 0.2 % SDS, 0.225 M Tris-HCl pH 6.8, 0.2 % TEMED, 0.1 % APS]. Before adding the APS and TEMED discard the 2-propanol layer covering the resolving gels and briefly rinse with water. Then pour the stacking solution containing APS and TEMED, and carefully place the comb avoiding bubbles. %s %s

steps.title: Take proper amount of protein (40-80...
steps.instruction: Take proper amount of protein (40-80 µg) add 2 µL of 5x Laemmli buffer and add mQH 2 O up to 10 µL. (5x Laemmli buffer: [80% (v/v) Glycerol, 20% (w/v) SDS, 20% (v/v) ß-Mercaptoethanol, 0.01% (w/v) bromophenol blue])

steps.title: Load samples and leave blank well...
steps.instruction: Load samples and leave blank well between samples to avoid contaminations.

steps.title: Run samples through stacking gel constant...
steps.instruction: Run samples through stacking gel constant 40 V, so until they reach stacking-resolving interphase.

steps.title: Run gel constant 80 V until...
steps.instruction: Run gel constant 80 V until bromophenol blue reach approximately 1 cm into the stacking gel.

steps.title: Immediately transfer the gel for staining
steps.instruction: Immediately transfer the gel for staining. Incubate 30 min in shaking. (Coomassie staining solution: 40% (v/v) methanol, 10% (v/v) acetic acid, 0.1% (w/v) Coomassie R-250, in distilled water. Dissolve the Coomassie in MeOH and then add the other components.]) Recovery of the tryptic peptides is dramatically reduced in over fixed gels

steps.title: Destain for 80 min replacing destain every 20 min
steps.instruction: Destain for 80 min replacing destain every 20 min. (Coomassie destaining solution: [40% (v/v) MeOH, 2% (v/v) acetic acid in mQH 2 O])

steps.title: Wash the gel in mQH 2 O for 30 min
steps.instruction: Wash the gel in mQH 2 O for 30 min

steps.title: Cut lanes out of the gel...
steps.instruction: Cut lanes out of the gel (can be stored at -20 °C for long term, before freezing cover with mQH 2 O)

steps.title: Chop the gel pieces with a...
steps.instruction: Chop the gel pieces with a scalpel over a glass plate. The final size of acrylamide pieces should be around 1 mm 3 . Glass plate should be carefully cleaned after each gel piece. Avoid taking gel without proteins.

steps.title: Transfer the gel pieces to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the gel pieces to a 1.5 mL LoBind tube and keep them on mQH 2 O until all samples are processed.

steps.title: Remove water and add 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Remove water and add 1 mL of 25 mM AmBic, incubate 15 min at 37 °C in a thermal shaker. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of 25 mM...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 25 mM AmBic, 50% ACN, incubate 15 min at 37 °C in a thermal shaker. Discard supernatant. Repeat ( The supernatant must be almost transparent or with a very light blue colour, if the blue colour is intense, repeat again. Band destaining and cleaning is crucial for a good LC-MS analysis. In these steps not only coomassie, but also SDS and other contaminants are removed. If there is still blue colour inside the gel pieces after three washing steps, continue with the protocol, it will not interfere with protein digestion. This usually happens when the gel pieces are not small enough.)

steps.title: Dehydrate gel pieces with 300 μL...
steps.instruction: Dehydrate gel pieces with 300 μL 100% ACN incubate 5 min at RT in a shaker. Discard supernatant. ( At this point gel pieces should be much reduced in volume, hard, and completely white, having a “plastic” look. If they are still translucent or soft repeat this step (you can use the pipette tip to test it).)

steps.title: Reduce disulphide bonds with 20 mM...
steps.instruction: Reduce disulphide bonds with 20 mM DTT in 100 mM AmBic (cover gel pieces) at 37 °C for 30 min.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of 100% ACN...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of 100% ACN to shrink gel pieces to remove DTT.

steps.title: Alkylate reduced Cys residues by adding...
steps.instruction: Alkylate reduced Cys residues by adding 55 mM IAA in 100 mM AmBic (cover gel pieces) at RT in DARK for 60min.

steps.title: Remove all IAA solution
steps.instruction: Remove all IAA solution

steps.title: Wash with 500 µL 25 mM...
steps.instruction: Wash with 500 µL 25 mM AmBic, incubate 15 min at 37 °C in a thermal shaker. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Wash with 25 mM AmBic, 50%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 25 mM AmBic, 50% ACN, incubate 15 min at 37 °C in a thermal shaker. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Dehydrate gel pieces with 300 μL...
steps.instruction: Dehydrate gel pieces with 300 μL 100% ACN incubate 5 min at RT in a shaker. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Dry out in a SpeedVac for 5 min
steps.instruction: Dry out in a SpeedVac for 5 min.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of Trypsin Solution...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of Trypsin Solution to each tube and incubate 15 minutes at 37 °C. If needed, add more trypsin solution until the gel pieces are completely rehydrated. Wait another 15 minutes. Completely cover the gel pieces with trypsin buffer.[Trypsin Solution: dilute Trypsin Sequencing Grade (Roche 11 418 475

steps.title: to 10-12
steps.instruction: to 10-12.5 ng/μL. Add 1 mL of trypsin buffer to the vial, incubate on ice for 10 min and then pipet up and down to completely resuspend the pellet. Transfer to a 2 mL LoBind tube. Add 1 mL of trypsin buffer to the vial, washing all the walls. Then transfer to the previous tube. Trypsin can be stored at -20 °C for 1 month. It is recommended to make aliquots to avoid melt/freezing cycles that can damage the enzyme. Aliquots should be stored in low binding tubes.];[ Trypsin Buffer : 25 mM NH 4 HCO 3 (AmBic), 10% (v/v) acetonitrile, 5 mM CaCl 2 . Calcium must be added in last place to avoid precipitation. Prepare fresh.]

steps.title: Incubate 14-16 h at 37 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate 14-16 h at 37 °C. Incubation in an oven is recommended, if a thermal block is used, cover it with aluminium foil to maintain the temperature also in the caps of the tubes. No shaking!

steps.title: Add 150 μL of 50% ACN,...
steps.instruction: Add 150 μL of 50% ACN, 1% FA to each tube with gel pieces and incubate 5 min at RT. (After incubation sonicate 3 min in a low intensity ultrasound bath.)

steps.title: Centrifuge (21000 g / 1 min)...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge (21000 g / 1 min) and transfer the supernatant to a new tube

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to the same tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to the same tube

steps.title: Add 100 μL of 90% ACN,...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of 90% ACN, 1% FA and incubate for 5 min at RT. Transfer the supernatant to the same tube again.

steps.title: Dry down in SpeedVac (same gradient...
steps.instruction: Dry down in SpeedVac (same gradient as for metabolites mentioned in step 3)

steps.title: Keep at -20 °C or proceed...
steps.instruction: Keep at -20 °C or proceed with desalting (long term storage at -80 °C)

steps.title: Add 75 μL of PS to peptide pellets
steps.instruction: Add 75 μL of PS to peptide pellets. Incubate for 10 min on ice. (Peptide Solubilisation Buffer (PS): [4% (v/v) ACN, 0.25% (v/v) FA])

steps.title: Re-suspend the pellet by pipetting and...
steps.instruction: Re-suspend the pellet by pipetting and sonicate 3 min in a low intensity ultrasound bath. Incubate 5 min at RT and then keep on ice.

steps.title: Use C18 stage tips (or 96-well plates for peptide desalting)
steps.instruction: Use C18 stage tips (or 96-well plates for peptide desalting). Independently of the chosen support the protocol remains unchanged (only volumes should be adjusted). All of the steps at RT.

steps.title: Plate activation with 400 µL MeOH...
steps.instruction: Plate activation with 400 µL MeOH x2, allow flowing to waste by gravity for 3 min then aspirate. (TIPS: 100 µL MeOH x2)

steps.title: Plate equilibration with 400 µL mQH...
steps.instruction: Plate equilibration with 400 µL mQH 2 O x4, allow to flow to waste by gravity for 3 min then aspirate. (TIPS: 100 µL mQH 2 O x2)

steps.title: Sample binding
steps.instruction: Sample binding: Pipette solution in the center of the well all of the volume in the tubes, including all of the insoluble parts that may remain in the solution. Confirm visually that the membrane absorbs the peptides. Allow to flow to waste by gravity for 10 min then aspirate. (TIPS: pipet slowly up and down)

steps.title: Sample desalting
steps.instruction: Sample desalting: with 400 μL of mQH 2 O to each well x

steps.title: Do not increase the vacuum too high. (TIPS
steps.instruction: Do not increase the vacuum too high. (TIPS: 100 µL mQH 2 O x

steps.title: Sample recovery
steps.instruction: Sample recovery: Replace the under well tray with a clean one. Elute peptides with 200 μL of MeOH x

steps.title: Allow to flow by gravity for >5 min then aspirate
steps.instruction: Allow to flow by gravity for >5 min then aspirate. (TIPS: 50 µL MeOH x2)

steps.title: Transfer the eluted peptides to LoBind tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer the eluted peptides to LoBind tubes.

steps.title: Dry down in speed-vac Peptides ready for LC-MS measurement
steps.instruction: Dry down in speed-vac Peptides ready for LC-MS measurement
